head.hi: Memorandum of Conversation, by the Secretary of State

div.p: Subject: Presentation by Madame Pandit of Prime Minister Nehru’s Personal Message of July 19, 1950

row.cell: Her Excellency, Madame Vijayalakshmi Pandit, Ambassador of India.
Mr. T. N. Kaul, First Secretary, Embassy of India.
The Honorable, Dean Acheson, Secretary of State.

div.p:
Madame Pandit handed me a note as she came in, saying that she did not wish to detain me long. I thanked her and read the note which was a reply from Prime Minister Jawaharlal NehruThe text of Mr. Nehru’s message is in telegram 148 from New Delhi, received on July 19 at 9:27 a. m., p. 425. to my message
of July 18, 1950, I told Madame Pandit that I very much appreciated her having brought the message to me and assured her that we would continue to give every serious consideration to the problems with which her brother is so actively concerned. I said that there were many grave issues in the world today but that I was confident that they could be resolved by the continued cooperation and determination of likeminded constructive nations such as India and the United States.
